name: Siouxland
description_short: Siouxland Federal Credit Union is a Nebraska-based credit union established in 1967, serving over 26,900 members with $319.4M in assets and a full range of personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Siouxland Federal Credit Union was established in 1967 and is headquartered in South Sioux City, Nebraska. As the 5th largest credit union in Nebraska, it has grown to serve over 26,900 members with $319.4 million in assets under management. The institution operates with approximately 71 dedicated employees and maintains a single branch location with 7 ATMs across the network.

Siouxland offers a comprehensive suite of banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRAs/KEOGH accounts, auto loans, personal loans, business loans, and credit cards. Members can access online banking, mobile payments, bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and drive-thru services. The credit union also provides financial counseling, financial education workshops, and student scholarships as community-focused initiatives.

The institution distinguishes itself through personalized member service and community engagement. Member reviews consistently highlight friendly, efficient staff who treat customers with respect and use personal names. Siouxland focuses on local geographic community service, enhancing financial accessibility for South Sioux City residents and regional businesses. Their membership base includes 28,800+ regular savings accounts, 14,600+ share draft accounts, 1,700+ money market accounts, and 2,400+ share certificates.

A notable limitation is the single branch location in South Sioux City with only 7 ATMs, restricting convenience for members outside the immediate South Sioux City area. Recent member reviews have raised concerns about newly installed automated ITM (In-The-Machine) drive-up teller machines being difficult to access. The institution's current lobby and drive-thru hours are listed as closed, though this may reflect a temporary closure window rather than permanent status.
services: Bill pay and online loan payments
Mobile payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements
pros: Established 57-year history (since 1967) with strong financial stability as the 5th largest credit union in Nebraska
$319.4 million in assets under management demonstrates solid economic footing
Comprehensive product range including auto loans, personal loans, business loans, CDs, money market accounts, and credit cards
Strong member satisfaction with 3.7/5 review rating; customers consistently praise personalized service and staff friendliness
Full-service digital banking: online banking, mobile app, remote deposit capture, mobile payments, and e-statements
Community-focused programs including financial counseling, financial education workshops, and student scholarships
Members report 10+ year loyalty with efficient service and respectful personal interactions
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for members outside South Sioux City area
Only 7 ATMs in network may create convenience issues for dispersed membership
Recent member complaints about newly installed automated ITM drive-up teller machines being difficult to use or access
Current lobby and drive-thru hours shown as closed, indicating potential service disruptions
Limited geographic footprint compared to larger regional or national banking institutions
best_for: South Sioux City and immediate surrounding Nebraska residents seeking community-focused credit union banking
Members prioritizing personalized service and local decision-making over larger institutional banking
Small business owners in the region needing business loans and commercial banking services
Consumers seeking financial education, counseling, and community engagement alongside banking services
diagnosis: Siouxland Federal Credit Union is best suited for South Sioux City-area residents and regional small business owners who value personalized service, community engagement, and local decision-making. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—with only one branch and 7 ATMs, this credit union works best for members with consistent access to the South Sioux City location.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
